description: Git and version control corrections. Force-push protection, secret detection, destructive operation guards.

corrections.what_went_wrong: Force-pushed to main/master or a shared branch
corrections.correct_approach: Never force-push to shared branches. Use feature branches. If you must force-push, confirm with the user and ensure no one else has pushed.
corrections.detection: git push --force, git push -f, targeting main/master

corrections.correct_approach: Check staged files for secrets before committing. Never stage .env, credentials.json, API keys, or token files.
corrections.detection: git add, git commit with sensitive file patterns

corrections.what_went_wrong: Used git reset --hard or checkout . and lost uncommitted work
corrections.correct_approach: Before any destructive git operation, check for uncommitted changes with git status. Stash or commit first.
corrections.detection: git reset --hard, git checkout ., git clean
